mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the long-term trend rate compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in high-GDP-growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.0 percent signals tight labor markets that boost wage-driven consumption and corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or neutral 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year backtest window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising short-term policy rates combined with accelerating headline inflation increase real discount rates, which mechanically depresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Real rate increases fail to produce negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of observations, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production alongside rising housing starts confirms broad-based capacity utilization, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined supply signal yields negative or flat 20-day forward returns in over 55 percent of triggered periods, or the interaction term loses statistical significance after controlling for demand proxies.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor demand that supports consumer spending and equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of cases, or the rank correlation between payroll changes and subsequent SPX returns remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in residential mortgage debt outstanding exceeding 1.5 percent signals expanding household leverage and housing market momentum, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Mortgage debt growth produces negative or neutral 60-day forward returns in more than 50 percent of triggered instances, or the signal shows no outperformance against a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising federal funds rates and falling unemployment compresses the policy slack buffer, triggering valuation multiple contraction and negative S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint rate-unemployment signal fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55 percent of observations, or single-series baselines consistently outperform the interaction model.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly contraction in housing starts below 500 thousand units indicates weakening consumer confidence and construction activity, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and yields negative S&P 500 returns over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Housing starts below the threshold produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances, or the signal exhibits no statistically significant predictive power over a 3-year rolling window.
